title: 10 best herbal teas for altitude sickness
intro_desc: Herbal teas for altitude sickness are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the symptoms of high-altitude maladies, such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue. These teas work by increasing oxygen levels in the body, improving circulation, and reducing inflammation. Examples include Andean tea, which contains glycerol and saponins to combat altitude sickness, and Tibetan tea, featuring Rhodiola rosea to boost energy and endurance. Drinking these teas can greatly improve lives for those living or traveling at high elevations, allowing them to adapt more easily to the challenges of altitude sickness and enjoy their surroundings with increased comfort and vitality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with altitude sickness because it contains a unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that help to improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the body. As altitude sickness is caused by a lack of oxygen reaching the body's tissues, the increased circulation provided by maidenhair tea can help to alleviate symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of the tea can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the lungs and brain, making it a natural remedy for high-altitude travelers.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Adiantum spp. contains antioxidants that can help protect the body against oxidative stress and inflammation, which are common consequences of high-altitude exposure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The plant's flavonoids and saponins have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue often associated with altitude sickness.

Vasodilatory properties: Adiantum spp. contains compounds that can help dilate blood vessels, improving oxygen delivery to the body's tissues and potentially reducing the risk of hypoxia (oxygen deficiency) at high altitudes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and improving oxygen delivery at high altitudes.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to improve blood flow and reduce platelet aggregation, which can alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for every eight ounces of water.
Heat the water in a teapot or kettle to boiling point for five minutes.
Remove from heat and steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in the hot water for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool for two minutes before drinking it slowly over a period of fifteen minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Golden root teas helps with altitude sickness because of its unique properties that help regulate blood flow and reduce inflammation in the body. The tea's active compounds, including isoflavones and saponins, work to constrict blood vessels, which can become dilated at high elevations, leading to symptoms like headaches and fatigue. Additionally, golden root's anti-inflammatory effects can help soothe any discomfort or pain associated with altitude sickness, making it a popular natural remedy for travelers and athletes ascending to higher elevations.
remedy_properties: Cardiovascular protection: Golden root tea's ability to improve cardiovascular function helps to reduce symptoms of high-altitude illnesses such as altitude sickness, by increasing blood flow and oxygen delivery to the body's tissues.

Antioxidant activity: Golden root tea's rich antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals that can accumulate in the body at high elevations, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with altitude sickness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Golden root tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and other tissues, which can alleviate symptoms of acute mountain sickness (AMS) such as coughing, chest tightness, and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Rhodiola rosea contain bioactive compounds that help to increase endurance and reduce fatigue caused by high altitudes.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of the plant have adaptogenic properties that can help the body adjust to changes in altitude.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried rhodiola rosea roots into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Heat filtered water to 212°f and pour it over the herb, allowing it to steep for 7-10 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup or container.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and stir well before serving hot.
Consume the tea immediately after preparation or store it in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng teas helps with altitude sickness because its unique properties help to increase oxygen availability to the body's tissues, allowing for better adaptation to high elevations. The tea's adaptogenic qualities also aid in reducing stress and fatigue caused by altitude changes, while its antioxidant compounds help to protect against oxidative damage from increased exposure to UV radiation at higher altitudes. By drinking Siberian ginseng teas regularly, individuals can experience improved acclimation and reduced symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Siberian Ginseng tea helps adapt to high-altitude conditions by reducing stress and improving overall resilience, allowing the body to better cope with low oxygen levels.

Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidants present in Siberian Ginseng tea help protect the body from damage caused by oxidative stress, which can exacerbate altitude sickness symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.

Cardiovascular Support: Siberian Ginseng tea's ability to improve cardiovascular function helps regulate blood pressure and flow, which is essential for proper oxygen delivery to tissues at high elevations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their rich content of bioactive compounds that help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and fatigue.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its adaptogenic properties, helping the body adapt to high-altitude conditions and reducing stress.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried eleutherococcus senticosus root powder approximately 2-3 grams.
Measure and combine one teaspoon of the root powder with boiling water for every cup desired.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the hot water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining solids and particles.
Serve the tea immediately while still warm or let it cool to room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng teas helps with altitude sickness because it increases oxygen delivery to the body's cells, reducing symptoms such as fatigue, headaches, and nausea. The herb's adaptogenic properties allow it to stimulate blood flow and improve circulation, helping the body acclimate to high elevations more efficiently. Additionally, ginseng tea's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress caused by increased altitude exposure, further supporting the body's natural response to altitude changes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginseng tea's antioxidants help to reduce oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions, which can exacerbate symptoms like headaches and fatigue.

Circulatory stimulation: The caffeine-like compounds in ginseng tea improve blood circulation and oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, helping to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as dizziness and nausea.

Adaptogenic properties: Ginseng tea has been shown to help the body adapt to changing environmental conditions, including high altitudes, by regulating stress hormones and promoting a sense of balance and well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ginseng roots are rich in compounds like ginsenosides and saponins, which help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and improving oxygen delivery to tissues.

Rhizomes: The rhizome is the underground stem of the ginseng plant, containing a higher concentration of active compounds than the roots, making it an effective ingredient for teas aimed at alleviating acute mountain sickness.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 2-3 grams of dried panax ginseng root in cold water for 30 seconds to remove impurities.
Boil 200ml of fresh water in a teapot or kettle for 5 minutes to ensure optimal temperature.
Steep the rinsed panax ginseng root in the boiling water for 10-15 minutes to allow for effective extraction.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids, then let it cool to room temperature.
Serve the cooled tea immediately, ideally within an hour of preparation, and consume 2-3 cups per day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai teas helps with altitude sickness because it contains dizziness-relieving properties that help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue. The tea's active compounds, including angelica, ligustilide, and butylidenephthalide, work to calm the nervous system, reduce inflammation, and improve circulation. This enables the body to adapt more effectively to high elevations, reducing the discomfort and distress associated with altitude sickness. By promoting balance and harmony in the body, dong quai teas provide natural relief from the challenges of climbing mountains or traveling to high-altitude areas.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dong Quai tea's antioxidants help protect against oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions, reducing damage to cells and tissues.

Cardiovascular-protective effects: Dong Quai tea's ability to dilate blood vessels and reduce blood pressure can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches, dizziness, and shortness of breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dong Quai tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the body caused by high-altitude exposure, which can contribute to conditions like acute mountain sickness (AMS).
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and improve oxygenation of tissues, making it effective in relieving symptoms of altitude sickness.
Rhyzomes: Contain a unique combination of compounds that help to stabilize blood pressure and promote cardiovascular health, reducing the risk of high-altitude hypoxia.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried angelica sinensis root per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes to allow for maximum extraction of beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large glass measuring cup.
Discard the solids and add honey or lemon to taste if desired then serve warm.
Allow the tea to cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with altitude sickness because it aids in digestion, which can be severely affected by high altitudes. As the air pressure changes, digestive issues such as nausea and bloating can occur. The menthol in peppermint tea soothes the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and cramping associated with altitude sickness. Additionally, peppermint's natural decongestant properties help to alleviate sinus congestion caused by high elevations, further alleviating symptoms of acute mountain sickness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and joint pain.
Digestive aid: The menthol content in peppermint tea helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing nausea and vomiting that are common complaints at high altitudes.

Vasodilatory properties: Peppermint's natural vasodilators help widen blood vessels, improving oxygen flow to the brain and other organs, which can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as dizziness and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain bioactive compounds like menthol and menthone, which help to reduce symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and nausea.
Barks: The barks of peppermint plants have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate respiratory issues caused by high altitudes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried peppermint leaves from a reliable source or purchase them at a local health food store.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Bring the measured amount of water to a boil in a tea kettle or pot and steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool for at least five minutes before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with altitude sickness because it contains compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the brain caused by high elevations. Additionally, its antioxidant-rich ingredients can combat oxidative stress and protect against damage to red blood cells, a common issue at high altitudes. The tea's vasodilatory effects also help widen blood vessels, improving oxygen delivery to the body's tissues and reducing symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and brain caused by high altitude, which can lead to altitude sickness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Kansas Coneflower tea can help neutralize free radicals generated by oxidative stress at high elevations, which can contribute to altitude-related symptoms such as fatigue and headaches.

Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds in Kansas Coneflower tea may help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion caused by high-altitude environments.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, making them a suitable choice for tea blends aimed at alleviating altitude sickness symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the plant, contain bioactive compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, which are common symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried echinacea angustifolia roots.
Bring 8 ounces of boiling water to a rapid simmer for 3-5 minutes.
Add the measured echinacea roots to the hot water and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking slowly throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with altitude sickness because it contains valerenic acid, which has been shown to improve sleep quality and reduce anxiety. As climbers ascend to high altitudes, they often experience disrupted sleep patterns and increased anxiety due to the decreased oxygen levels. Valerian tea's calming effects can help alleviate these symptoms, allowing climbers to rest better and cope with the physical demands of high-altitude trekking. Additionally, valerenic acid has been found to increase nitric oxide production in the body, which may also help improve oxygen delivery to tissues at high elevations.
remedy_properties: Sedative effects: Valerian tea's sedative properties can help alleviate symptoms of insomnia, anxiety, and restlessness often experienced at high altitudes, promoting a good night's sleep and reducing stress.

Vasodilatory effects: Valerian tea's ability to dilate blood vessels can help reduce blood pressure and increase oxygen delivery to the brain and other vital organs, which can be compromised at high elevations.
Antioxidant properties: Valerian tea's rich antioxidant content can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation caused by altitude sickness, which can damage tissues and organs.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain valerenic acid, which has been shown to help reduce symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes have a calming effect on the body, making them useful for reducing anxiety and stress caused by high altitudes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried valeriana officinalis roots and leaves and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 8 ounces of water in a kettle or pot for 3-5 minutes before steeping the herbs.
Add the boiled water to the cup containing the valeriana officinalis mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another heat-resistant cup, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, ideally within 30-40 minutes of preparation for maximum efficacy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with altitude sickness because its calming properties can soothe the body's response to high elevations. The tea's natural flavonoids and antioxidants help to reduce inflammation, which is a common symptom of altitude sickness. Additionally, the tea's ability to promote relaxation can aid in reducing stress and anxiety that often accompany altitude sickness, allowing the body to adapt more efficiently to the changed environment. This herbal remedy can provide relief from symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Lavender tea's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions, which can exacerbate symptoms like headaches and fatigue.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory properties in lavender tea may help alleviate symptoms such as joint pain and swelling associated with acute mountain sickness (AMS).
Calming effects on the nervous system: Lavender's calming effects can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common complaints among individuals experiencing AMS, and promote relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by high altitudes.
Seeds: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, seeds aid in relieving symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming it, ideally within 10-15 minutes of preparation.
Consume 2-3 cups of the herbal tea per day, spaced evenly throughout the day for optimal relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mongolian dandelion teas helps with altitude sickness because of its unique blend of natural compounds that work together to alleviate symptoms. The tea's high concentration of inulin, a prebiotic fiber, helps to regulate gut health and reduce inflammation, which can be triggered by the decrease in oxygen levels at high elevations. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties help to counteract oxidative stress caused by altitude exposure, promoting overall well-being and reducing discomfort associated with mountain sickness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: The high antioxidant content in Mongolian Dandelion tea helps to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation caused by high-altitude exposure, which can exacerbate symptoms of altitude sickness.

Diuretic and Detoxifying Effects: The diuretic properties of the tea help to remove excess fluids and toxins from the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue associated with altitude sickness.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Mongolian Dandalion tea may help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and brain caused by high-altitude exposure, which can contribute to more severe forms of altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Mongolian dandelion plant contain inulin, a natural compound that helps to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function, making them effective against altitude sickness.
Buds: The buds of the Mongolian dandelion plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and nausea, common symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum mongolicum leaves and flowers in the early morning for optimal potency.
Rinse the herb material with filtered water to remove impurities and debris gently.
Measure out 10 grams of dried taraxacum mongolicum herb or 20-30 grams of fresh material per liter of boiling water carefully.
Steep the herb material in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot promptly.
Allow the tea to cool and settle for at least 10-15 minutes before serving, and enjoy as needed daily.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat altitude sickness with herbal teas is by sipping on a calming brew of peppermint or chamomile tea. These teas help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, easing symptoms of high-altitude headaches and fatigue. Additionally, ginger tea can aid in digestion issues that often accompany altitude changes. Drink 1-2 cups daily to alleviate discomfort and accelerate acclimation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to altitude sickness that are treated with herbal teas are symptoms associated with low oxygen levels, such as fatigue, headaches, and dizziness. These include conditions like hypoxia, anemia, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Herbal teas like ginseng, yerba mate, and peppermint may help alleviate these symptoms by improving circulation, reducing inflammation, and increasing oxygenation of the body.
